name: pull-requests
context: Create, review, and merge pull requests via the GitHub API
info: This skill covers the full pull request lifecycle: creating PRs from feature branches or forks, requesting and submitting reviews, checking CI status from [[github/actions]], and merging via merge commit, squash, or rebase. All operations use the GitHub REST API via @octokit/rest. Agents use this skill to propose code changes, participate in code review, and land changes into protected branches. PRs can reference [[github/issues]] with closing keywords. See [[github/auth]] for authentication and [[github/repos#branch-protection]] for merge requirements.

sections.title: Creating pull requests
sections.body: Use `octokit.pulls.create()` with `owner`, `repo`, `title`, `body`, `head` (source branch), and `base` (target branch). Set `draft: true` for work-in-progress PRs. Use closing keywords in the body (`Fixes #42`) to auto-close linked [[github/issues]] on merge. See [[github/auth]] for token setup.

sections.title: Review and approval
sections.body: Request reviews with `octokit.pulls.requestReviewers()`. Submit reviews with `octokit.pulls.createReview()` using events: `APPROVE`, `REQUEST_CHANGES`, or `COMMENT`. Add inline comments via the `comments` array in `createReview()`. Branch protection rules in [[github/repos#branch-protection]] can require approvals before merge.

sections.title: Merge strategies
sections.body: Three merge methods: `merge` (preserves history, adds merge commit), `squash` (single commit, clean history), `rebase` (linear history, no merge commit). Configure allowed methods per-repo via [[github/repos#settings]]. Squash is preferred for feature branches. Rebase for small, atomic changes.

sections.title: CI checks and status
sections.body: Check CI status with `octokit.repos.getCombinedStatusForRef()` (commit statuses) and `octokit.checks.listForRef()` ([[github/actions]] check runs). Combined state is `success`, `failure`, or `pending`. Branch protection can require specific checks to pass before merge. See [[github/actions#workflow-syntax]] for defining CI workflows.
